Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Microgpt explained interactively 271 upvotes, 37 comments
  2. Decision trees – the unreasonable power of nested decision rules 481 upvotes, 74 comments
  3. Computer-generated dream world: Virtual reality for a 286 processor 101 upvotes, 13 comments
  4. Show HN: Timber – Ollama for classical ML models, 336x faster than Python 124 upvotes, 20 comments
  5. Making Video Games in 2025 (without an engine) 101 upvotes, 16 comments
  6. Flightradar24 for Ships 228 upvotes, 46 comments
  7. Everett shuts down Flock camera network after judge rules footage public record 230 upvotes, 49 comments
  8. Right-sizes LLM models to your system's RAM, CPU, and GPU 136 upvotes, 31 comments
  9. Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS Foundation 415 upvotes, 130 comments
  10. The Mini PET 40/80 (2022) 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. Ghostty – Terminal Emulator 732 upvotes, 311 comments
  12. Have your cake and decompress it too 18 upvotes, 3 comments
  13. Next-gen spacecraft are overwhelming communication networks 64 upvotes, 21 comments
  14. Show HN: Omni – Open-source workplace search and chat, built on Postgres 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Tove Jansson's criticized illustrations of The Hobbit (2023) 175 upvotes, 80 comments
  16. WebMCP is available for early preview 281 upvotes, 157 comments
  17. How to record and retrieve anything you've ever had to look up twice 46 upvotes, 19 comments
  18. Little Free Library 117 upvotes, 63 comments
  19. When does MCP make sense vs CLI? 375 upvotes, 242 comments
  20. Enable CORS for Your Blog 32 upvotes, 14 comments
  21. Ape Coding [fiction] 175 upvotes, 118 comments
  22. Why XML tags are so fundamental to Claude 202 upvotes, 139 comments
  23. An interactive intro to Elliptic Curve Cryptography 17 upvotes, 7 comments
  24. Why does C have the best file API 111 upvotes, 83 comments
  25. Process-Based Concurrency: Why Beam and OTP Keep Being Right 30 upvotes, 20 comments
  26. If AI writes code, should the session be part of the commit? 233 upvotes, 227 comments
  27. Evolving descriptive text of mental content from human brain activity 22 upvotes, 14 comments
  28. Setting up phones is a nightmare 153 upvotes, 194 comments
  29. Long Range E-Bike (2021) 162 upvotes, 246 comments
  30. How to talk to anyone and why you should 15 upvotes, 258 comments
